title: Southern California is being paid up to $65 million to leave 65 billion gallons of water for it
summary_full: Historically low water levels are plaguing Lake Mead, affecting millions in various states and Mexico. To combat this, federal funds will support efforts to keep water in the reservoir this year, coupled with initiatives aimed at conserving agricultural water annually. While these measures are crucial for now, achieving lasting change will necessitate cooperation among Colorado River Basin states regarding future water management.
